generatedSentence: An outcast lived near a rickety outpost, observing the vultures that would veer overhead. His naivety was a cloak, hiding a nascent desire for change. He often felt abashed by his simple life, yet understood it was necessary to acknowledge his circumstances. His small vessel was his only escape, though it offered neither comfort nor safety, a dowdy thing built for a single purpose. He dreamed of a journey beyond the offshore horizon. One night, a freakish storm struck, turning the serene violet sky into a maelstrom of torment. The undertow threatened to drown his dreams, and the temperature dropped to a perilous low. He saw a flicker of light, an impressive demonstration of a distant utility beacon, but a bottleneck of debris blocked his path. This was the nadir of his courage, yet he knew to acquire what he sought, this ordeal must be undertaken. The thought of his downfall was palpable. With a surge of insanity, he grabbed a jackhammer from his repository, its vibrations a primal agony. He had to bolt past a baleful jaguar that seemed to fly from the shadows, its eyes like jargoon. His insurance against failure was sheer will, as he felt the tether of his past trying to hold him back. The breakneck pace of his escape was exhilarating, a desperate act to reverse his fate, an unearthing of hidden strength. The theater of his mind played out a vivid struggle. The storm vanished, leaving behind a tender dawn. He felt allowed to finally shed his past, no longer a runaway nor a bumpkin lost in his insular thoughts. The freakish journey had forged him. He reached an ultrathin spire, an informative structure, its jackstraw-like antennae reaching for the sky, and there, as if by magic, a simple dinner awaited him. He sent his regards to the past, feeling his spirit truly beginning to fly with every flicker of hope, even sensing a faint onions scent from a nearby garden.
panelSentences: An outcast lived near a rickety outpost, observing the vultures that would veer overhead. His naivety was a cloak, hiding a nascent desire for change. One night, a freakish storm struck, turning the serene violet sky into a maelstrom of torment. The undertow threatened to drown his dreams, and the temperature dropped to a perilous low. He saw a flicker of light, an impressive demonstration of a distant utility beacon, but a bottleneck of debris blocked his path. This was the nadir of his courage, yet he knew to acquire what he sought, this ordeal must be undertaken. The thought of his downfall was palpable.

With a surge of insanity, he grabbed a jackhammer from his repository, its vibrations a primal agony. He had to bolt past a baleful jaguar that seemed to fly from the shadows, its eyes like jargoon. His insurance against failure was sheer will, as he felt the tether of his past trying to hold him back. The breakneck pace of his escape was exhilarating, a desperate act to reverse his fate, an unearthing of hidden strength. The theater of his mind played out a vivid struggle.

The storm vanished, leaving behind a tender dawn. He felt allowed to finally shed his past, no longer a runaway nor a bumpkin lost in his insular thoughts. The freakish journey had forged him. He reached an ultrathin spire, an informative structure, its jackstraw-like antennae reaching for the sky, and there, as if by magic, a simple dinner awaited him. rawPanelSentences: An **outcast** lived near a **rickety outpost**, observing the **vultures** that would **veer** overhead. His **naivety** was a cloak, hiding a **nascent** desire for change. He often felt **abashed** by his simple life, yet understood it was **necessary** to **acknowledge** his circumstances. His small **vessel** was his only escape, though it offered **neither** comfort nor safety, a **dowdy** thing built for a single purpose. He dreamed of a **journey** beyond the **offshore** horizon.

One night, a **freakish** storm struck, turning the serene **violet** sky into a maelstrom of **torment**. The **undertow** threatened to **drown** his dreams, and the **temperature** dropped to a perilous low. He saw a **flicker** of light, an **impressive demonstration** of a distant **utility** beacon, but a **bottleneck** of debris blocked his path. This was the **nadir** of his courage, yet he knew to **acquire** what he sought, this ordeal must be **undertaken**. The thought of his **downfall** was palpable.

With a surge of **insanity**, he grabbed a **jackhammer** from his **repository**, its vibrations a primal **agony**. He had to **bolt** past a **baleful jaguar** that seemed to **fly** from the shadows, its eyes like **jargoon**. His **insurance** against failure was sheer will, as he felt the **tether** of his past trying to hold him back. The **breakneck** pace of his escape was exhilarating, a desperate act to **reverse** his fate, an **unearthing** of hidden strength. The **theater** of his mind played out a vivid struggle.

The storm **vanished**, leaving behind a **tender** dawn. He felt **allowed** to finally shed his past, no longer a **runaway** nor a **bumpkin** lost in his **insular** thoughts. The **freakish** journey had forged him. He reached an **ultrathin** spire, an **informative** structure, its **jackstraw**-like antennae reaching for the sky, and there, as if by magic, a simple **dinner** awaited him. He sent his **regards** to the past, feeling his spirit truly beginning to **fly** with every **flicker** of hope, even sensing a faint **onions** scent from a nearby garden.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, retro sci-fi adventure style, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A lone explorer wearing a tattered, deep-sea diving suit, with glowing emerald visors and a weathered, metallic backpack. cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The lone explorer standing on a rickety, wind-battered outpost, gazing out at a dark, turbulent offshore ocean under a purple twilight sky, with three large vultures circling ominously in the distance. Wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The lone explorer's small, dowdy vessel being violently tossed by colossal waves in a dark, stormy ocean, debris forming a dangerous bottleneck, while a faint flicker of light emanates from a distant, towering utility beacon. Medium shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The lone explorer, wielding a heavy jackhammer, in a breakneck sprint through a dark, metallic repository, desperately trying to bolt past a snarling, baleful jaguar with glowing jargoon-like eyes, surrounded by ancient machinery. Dynamic low-angle shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The lone explorer standing triumphantly atop an ultrathin, intricate spire, arms outstretched towards a tender, dawning sky as storm clouds vanish, overlooking a vast, peaceful landscape with distant, subtle onion fields. High-angle shot.

definitions.example: The home side won in a runaway.

definitions.definition: Having run away; escaped; fugitive
definitions.example: a runaway thief

definitions.definition: Easily won, as a contest
definitions.example: a runaway victory at the polls

definitions.definition: Unchecked; rampant
definitions.example: runaway prices

definitions.definition: Deserting or revolting against one's group, duties, expected conduct, or the like, especially to establish or join a rival group, change one's life drastically, etc.
definitions.example: The runaway delegates nominated their own candidate.

definitions.definition: The opposite of something.
definitions.example: We believed the Chinese weren't ready for us. In fact, the reverse was true.

definitions.definition: The act of going backwards; a reversal.
A piece of misfortune; a setback.
The tails side of a coin, or the side of a medal or badge that is opposite the obverse.
The side of something facing away from a viewer, or from what is considered the front; the other side.
The gear setting of an automobile that makes it travel backwards.
A thrust in fencing made with a backward turn of the hand; a backhanded stroke.
A turn or fold made in bandaging, by which the direction of the bandage is changed.
To turn something around so that it faces the opposite direction or runs in the opposite sequence.
definitions.example: to reverse a portion of video footage

definitions.definition: To turn something inside out or upside down.
To transpose the positions of two things.
To change totally; to alter to the opposite.
To return, come back.
To turn away; to cause to depart.
To cause to return; to recall.
To revoke a law, or to change a decision into its opposite.
definitions.example: to reverse a judgment, sentence, or decree

definitions.definition: To cause a mechanism or a vehicle to operate or move in the opposite direction to normal.
To change the direction of a reaction such that the products become the reactants and vice-versa.
To place a set of points in the reverse position
(of points) to move from the normal position to the reverse position
To overthrow; to subvert.
Opposite, contrary; going in the opposite direction.
definitions.example: The mirror showed us a reverse view of the scene.

definitions.definition: Pertaining to engines, vehicle movement etc. moving in a direction opposite to the usual direction.
definitions.example: He selected reverse gear.

definitions.definition: (of points) To be in the non-default position; to be set for the lesser-used route.
Turned upside down; greatly disturbed.
Reversed.
definitions.example: a reverse shell

definitions.definition: In which cDNA synthetization is obtained from an RNA template.
In a reverse way or direction; in reverse; upside-down.
Of an object: not strong or sturdy, as because of poor construction or upkeep; not safe or secure.
definitions.example: He hesitated about climbing such a small, rickety ladder.

definitions.definition: Of a person: feeble in the joints; tottering.
definitions.example: The rickety old man hardly managed to climb the stairs.

definitions.definition: Affected with or suffering from rickets.
A military post stationed at a distance from the main body of troops.
definitions.example: The outpost did not have enough ammunition to resist a determined assault.

definitions.definition: The body of troops manning such a post.
definitions.example: Sgt. Smith fleeced most of the rest of the outpost of their earnings in their weekly game of craps.

definitions.definition: An outlying settlement.
definitions.example: Beyond the border proper, there are three small outposts not officially under government protection.

definitions.definition: A square protected by a pawn that is in or near the enemy's stronghold.
A monocotyledonous plant (Allium cepa), allied to garlic, used as vegetable and spice.
The bulb of such a plant.
The genus as a whole.
A ball.
A person from Bermuda or of Bermudian descent.
To cast out; to banish.
That has been cast out; banished, ostracized.
An area or or portion of sea away from the shore.
An island, outcrop, or other land away from shore.
Something or someone in, from, or associated with another country.
To use foreign labor to substitute for local labor.
Moving away from the shore.
Located in the sea away from the coast.
definitions.example: an offshore oil rig

definitions.definition: Located in another country, especially one having beneficial tax laws.
Away from the shore.
At some distance from the shore.
